source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A uniform sheet of surface current of strength $\lambda$ (ampères per meter in the $y$ direction) flows eastward (in $x$ direction) on a horizontal plane ($z=0$), as shown in Fig. 2.50. What are the magnitude and direction of the force on: The same segment but oriented so as to carry a current in the westward direction? State briefly the reason for each of your answers.

metadata.reasoning_chain: The current element is $i\,d=-i\,l\,\mathbf{e}_{x}$ and the force is calculated as $\mathbf{F}=\frac{\mu_{0} i l \lambda}{\pi}\arctan\left(\frac{L}{R}\right)\mathbf{e}_{x}\times\mathbf{e}_{y}=\frac{\mu_{0} l i \lambda}{\pi}\arctan\left(\frac{L}{R}\right)\mathbf{e}_{z}$.
